evidence_excerpt: Patient 1’s muscle weakness gradually progressed; he became wheelchair-bound at age 24 and developed chronic renal failure requiring periodic dialysis, with mildly decreased cardiac function from age 22.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing progressive myopathy and persistent thrombocytopenia with major complications (dialysis for renal failure and mild cardiac insufficiency); no resolution reported.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: He has been wheelchair-bound since the age of 24 years.
Kidney disease later progressed to chronic renal failure requiring periodic dialysis therapy.
From the age of 22 years, he has had mildly decreased cardiac function accompanied by mitral insufficiency.

notes_on_uncertainty: The case report includes two siblings; timing of the definitive GNE myopathy diagnosis is not explicitly dated. Ages were used to approximate events relative to diagnosis (e.g., biopsy/genetic confirmation around age 20 for Patient 1). Events are primarily based on Patient 1’s course; Patient 2 remains ambulatory with a walking aid but detailed post-diagnosis timing is unclear.
